english: the tree (he climbs) Dzl.; ču k'ar (chu khar) on the water; p'yogs bźi k'á-ru (phyogs bzhi kha ru) all round Glr. 2. on, at, ču k'ar (chu khar) on the river side, mt'so k'ar (mtsho khar) p'ebs (phebs) he came to the lake Pth. 3. above, besides, = stéń-du (steng du) Mil. 4. towards, in the face of, mt'son k'ar sra (mtshon khar sra) proof against thrust or blow Mil. 5. at the time of, when, sléb-pai k'ar (sleb pai khar), sleb k'ar (sleb khar), byon k'ar (byon khar) when (he) arrived; ré-bai k'ar (re bai khar) in the hope of; — k'á-nas (kha nas) down from, away from, rta k'á-nas ‘bébs-pa (rta kha nas 'bebs pa) to alight from the horse Glr.; *k'á-na (kha na), k'á-ne (kha ne), k'á-la* (kha la) col. for sgó-nas (sgo nas), *t'abs-si k'á-na* (thabs si kha na) by way of the opportunity, on occasion, *yun riń-gi k'á-ne* (yun ring gi kha ne) by little and little, gradually. — 5. sharpness, edge, of a knife etc., *k'a t'ug-po soń* (kha thug po song) the edge has become blunt, *log soń* (log song) has become bad; *k'a mi dug* (kha mi dug) the edge is wanting; meï (me'i), čuï (chu'i), rluń-gi k'a nón-pa (rlung gi kha non pa) to suppress the sharpness of the fire, water, wind, to stop the flames, floods etc. (viz. by means of incantations) Glr.; *k'a tón-če (kha ton ce), p'in-če* (phin ce) W. to grind, to sharpen; k'a lén-pa (kha len pa) to become sharp Sch. V. yesterday, also: the day before yesterday, k'aï (kha'i), nyin (nyin) id., cf. k'a-rtsań (kha rtsang). Compounds. k'a-dkri (kha dkri) (C. *-ṭi* (-Ti), W. *-śri* (-shri)) neck-cloth, sometimes worn as a protection against cold. — k'á-skoń (kha skong), k'as-skoń (khas skong) appendix, of a book. — k'a-skyur-po (kha skyur po) olive, olive-tree Sik. — k'a-k'a-sáń (kha kha sang) or siń (sing) about two months ago C. — k'a-k'ébs (kha khebs) cover, lid Sch. — k'a-kór (kha kor), k'a-k'yér (kha khyer) border Sch. — k'a-k'ral (kha khral) Cs.: respect, regard, with respect to. — k'a-k'ór (kha khor) the circumference of the mouth Cs. — k'a-gáń (kha gang) (cf. k'a III (kha III)) quadrate, square, k'a-gáń-ba (kha gang ba) square adj., k'a-gáń-ma (kha gang ma) id., e.g. pieces of cloth so shaped. — k'a-gáb (kha gab) Sch. cover, lid. — k'a-góń (kha gong) snow-ball. — k'a-grú (kha gru) corner of the mouth. — k'a-mgál (kha mgal) v. k'a-só (kha so). — k'a-rgán (kha rgan) Mil. privilege of old age n. f. — k'a-rgód (kha rgod) Sch.: ill language; a slanderer. k'a-rgyúg (kha rgyug) Glr. acc. to the context: idle talk, unfounded assertion. — k'a-rgyúd (kha rgyud) or -gyún (-gyun), resp. źal-rgyun (zhal rgyun), oral tradition, esp. certain mystical doctrines not allowed to be written down. — k'a-bsgós (kha bsgos) advice, = k'á-ta (kha ta); commandment, cf. bka-bsgós (bka' bsgos). — k'a-mńár (kha mngar) bitter and sweet. — k'a-čig (kha cig) (v. k'a III (kha III)) some, — k'a-byáń (kha byang) clever talking, cf. k'a sbyáń-po (kha sbyang po) eloquent. (Cs.: fair words?) — k'a-gčód (kha gcod) cover, lid; cork. — k'a-bčól (kha bcol) Sch. idle talk, prattle. — k'a-čág (kha chag) Mil., was explained: abuse, ill language. — k'a-čád (kha chad), resp. źal-čád (zhal chad) agreement, convention, covenant, *k'. zúm-če* (kh. zum ce) W. to conclude a convention. — k'a-čár (kha char) Mil. snow and rain; k'a-ma-čár (kha ma char) both falling promiscuously, sleet. — k'a-číńs (kha chings) the appeasing of wild beasts etc. by witchcraft Mil. — k'a-čú (kha chu) 1. spittle Cs. 2. snow-water. — k'a-čé (kha che) 1. a large mouth. 2. a person that has to command over much (cf. k'a-drág (kha drag), k'a-źán (kha zhan)). 3. n. of a mask in the religious plays. 4. n. of a country, Cashmere, v. below. — k'a-čéms (kha chems) last will, k'a-čéms jóg-pa (kha chems jog pa) to make a testament. — k'a-čós (kha chos) hypocrisy. — k'a-mčú (kha mchu) 1. lip. 2. Sch.: word, voice (?) 3. quarrel, dispute. — k'a-rǰé (kha rje) 1. great lord, mighty personage Cs. (?) 2. good luck, good fortune Cs.; but in C. it is only used for fortune = goods, wealth. — k'a-ñuń (kha nyung) Sch sparing of words, laconic. — k'á-ta (kha ta), also k'á-lta (kha lta) good advice, lesson, byéd-pa (byed pa) or jóg-pa (jog pa) to give, C. W. — k'a-tód-la (kha tod la) (or -na (-na)) Ld. = k'a-tóg la (kha tog la), on, upon. — k'a-tón (kha ton) Cs.: 'a reading or saying with a loud voice' (Lex. पठन), better: the saying by heart, klóg-gam k'a-tón-du dón-nas (klog gam kha ton du don nas) reading or saying by heart, k'a-tón-du śés-pa (kha ton du shes pa) to know by heart Dzl.; gen. in reference to religious texts. — k'a-gtám (kha gtam) Cs. tradition. — k'a-stóń (kha stong) not yet having eaten anything. — k'a-t'úg (kha thug) C. to the brim. — k'a-tóg-la (kha tog la) or -na (-na), = k'á-la (kha la), above, upon, on the top or surface cf, k'a-tóg-tu (kha tog tu) id.; k'a-tóg-nas (kha tog nas) down from. — k'a-tór (kha tor) Sch. pustules in the mouth. — k'a-dig (kha dig), k'a-ldig-mk'an (kha ldig mkhan) W. stammerer. — k'a-dóg (kha dog), also k'a (kha) (v. k'a IV. 4. (kha IV. 4.)) colour skra mťoń-mtiń-gi k'a-dóg-tu gyúr-to (skra mthong mthing gi kha dog tu gyur to) the hair became blue Dzl.; k'a sgyur-ba (kha sgyur ba) to change colour, k'a gyur (kha gyur) the colour changes, cf. also mdog (mdog). — k'a - drág (kha - drag) 1. mighty. 2 haughty. — k'a-drań (kha drang) W. over-against, just before, opposite, straight on. — k'a-śdáms (kha shdams), = k'á-ta (kha ta), ydúms-k'a (ydums kha), advice W. — k'a-dúr (kha dur)
